toolCalls.name: update_topic

args.title: Fix Landing Page Auth State
args.summary: I investigated the issue where the landing page was showing the 'Get Started' and 'Sign In' buttons to users even if they were logged in. I updated the `client/app/page.tsx` server component to fetch the user's authentication state via Supabase and pass an `isLoggedIn` boolean to the `LandingPageClient`. Then, I modified `LandingPageClient` to accept this prop and conditionally render a 'Go to Dashboard' button in the navigation header and hero section when the user is logged in. Finally, I successfully ran a build to ensure the code changes compile and type-check properly.
args.strategic_intent: Completed fixing the landing page auth state.

content:
I have successfully updated the landing page so it correctly reflects the authentication state. 

Here is what was changed:
1. Updated `client/app/page.tsx` to check the Supabase auth state and pass an `isLoggedIn` prop to the landing page client component.
2. Modified `client/components/landing/landing-page-client.tsx` to accept the `isLoggedIn` prop.
3. Updated the Navigation Header and the Hero section inside `LandingPageClient` to display a **"Go to Dashboard"** button instead of the **"Sign In"** and **"Get Started"** / **"Start Bookmarking Free"** buttons if the user is already authenticated. 

I've also verified the application compiles and builds successfully with these changes.

args.strategic_intent: Finished implementing the loading skeleton state in the dashboard.
args.title: Dashboard Loading State
args.summary: I added an `isLoading` state to the `DashboardClient` component that is initially true and set to false once `fetchInitialData` is complete. When `isLoading` is true, the vault section renders a grid of 6 pulsing skeleton cards matching the appearance of the actual bookmark cards. This gives the user immediate visual feedback while data arrives and prevents the screen from appearing empty. Lastly, I successfully ran a build to ensure the project still compiles.

response.output: Strategic Intent: Finished implementing the loading skeleton state in the dashboard.

toolCalls.resultDisplay:
> [!STRATEGY]
> **Intent:** Finished implementing the loading skeleton state in the dashboard.

response.output: Strategic Intent: Finished implementing the loading skeleton state in the dashboard.

content:
I've successfully added a loading skeleton animation to the dashboard. 

Here is what was changed:
1. Added an `isLoading` state inside `client/components/dashboard/dashboard-client.tsx` that defaults to `true` and turns to `false` when the initial Supabase data fetch completes.
2. Updated the Vault tab's render logic to show a grid of beautifully styled, pulsing skeleton cards (matching your UI theme) while `isLoading` is active.
3. Updated the reference count to read "Loading..." while the network request is still pending.

Now, instead of seeing an empty list for a brief second before the data loads, users will see the skeleton loader simulating the layout of incoming cards.
